description: Fix the Confluence plugin's auth detection to use the correct meta tag for Atlassian account ID instead of the username meta tag.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/confluence && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Use ajs-atlassian-account-id meta tag instead of ajs-remote-user
userStories.description: The getAuth function reads 'ajs-remote-user' which contains the username/display name, not the Atlassian account ID needed for API v2 calls.
acceptanceCriteria: getAuth reads from meta tag 'ajs-atlassian-account-id' for the accountId field
The auth detection still returns null when the user is not logged in (the meta tag is absent)
If ajs-atlassian-account-id is not available, falls back to ajs-remote-user for backwards compatibility with older Confluence versions
userStories.notes:
Files:
- plugins/confluence/src/confluence-api.ts (fix getAuth)

Context:
The `getAuth` function reads the account ID from the wrong meta tag (line 22):
```typescript
const accountId = getMetaContent('ajs-remote-user');
```

`ajs-remote-user` contains the user's display name or legacy username on Confluence Cloud, NOT the Atlassian account ID (which looks like `5b10a2844c20165700ede21g`).

The correct meta tag is `ajs-atlassian-account-id`, which the Jira plugin uses correctly.

Fix:
```typescript
const accountId = getMetaContent('ajs-atlassian-account-id') ?? getMetaContent('ajs-remote-user');
```

The fallback to ajs-remote-user handles Confluence Server/Data Center which may not have the Atlassian account ID meta tag.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the baseUrl or cloudId extraction
- Do NOT change the ConfluenceAuth interface
- Keep the null check: `if (!accountId || !baseUrl) return null;`
